title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale its GTM operational backbone. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration expertise, and the ability to collaborate across globally distributed teams including the Tel Aviv HQ. A builder's mentality and strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR are essential.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS expertise aligns perfectly, Salesforce admin capability confirmed, scaling experience well-suited to high-growth mandate, and Tel Aviv HQ location matches candidate's preferred geography.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations opening and immediately thought of your background—scaling RevOps operations in high-growth B2B SaaS environments is exactly what you've built your career around. Given your 15+ years in GTM and deep Salesforce expertise, plus your base in Tel Aviv, this role reporting to the CRO to build the operational backbone for a company trusted by The New York Times and Wiz seems like a natural fit. Would be great to explore how your experience scaling beyond $30M ARR could accelerate Finout's next growth phase.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong seeks a RevOps Manager to redesign territory models and processes for global scalability. The role partners with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition, conducting quantitative analyses to align territories with business goals. Requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, strong analytical skills in Excel/SQL/BI tools (Tableau, Retool), and proficiency with Salesforce and territory management tools like Fullcast, Pigment, or Anaplan. Salary range $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Exceptional match—Gong is a RevOps-native company and this role perfectly aligns with Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS GTM, territory optimization, and Salesforce expertise. The analytical depth (Excel/SQL/BI tools, pipeline forecasting) and focus on scaling sales operations are core strengths.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building scalable GTM operations in B2B SaaS, and I've always respected how Gong weaponizes data for revenue teams. Your Territory Strategy & Operations role caught my attention because territory modeling and sales process optimization are exactly where I've driven the most revenue impact. I'd love to explore how my background in Salesforce-driven territory management and pipeline architecture could help you redesign Gong's global territory composition.

title: Revenue Operations Manager - Systems
location: New York / San Francisco / Salt Lake City (Hybrid) or Remote
description: Flex seeks a Revenue Operations Manager to build and own the Salesforce environment for its New Verticals division, including objects, flows, and automations. Requires 5+ years in Revenue/Sales/GTM Operations with at least 2 years in a senior or lead capacity, deep Salesforce expertise (Admin Certification preferred), and experience standing up RevOps infrastructure for a scaling business unit. Familiarity with GTM systems such as Outreach, LeanData, Clay, and HubSpot is needed. A builder mentality energized by creating from zero is essential.
score_reason: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps with deep Salesforce expertise and proven track record building GTM infrastructure from zero aligns perfectly with Flex's need to own the Salesforce environment for New Verticals. Remote option and seniority level make this an excellent match despite being slightly above the role's stated 5+ years requirement.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years architecting RevOps infrastructure for scaling B2B SaaS companies, including building Salesforce environments from the ground up with complex automations, custom objects, and GTM system integrations. Flex's mission to expand into new verticals with a dedicated RevOps foundation resonates with my passion for creating operational leverage at inflection points—I'd love to discuss how I can accelerate your New Verticals division's go-to-market velocity.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Redwood Software, the first SaaS-based composable automation platform for ERP, seeks a GTM Engineer to lead the design, implementation, and optimization of AI-driven tools and automations across marketing. Requires 5+ years in marketing operations/martech/demand gen with B2B GTM understanding, deep knowledge of MarTech platforms (Salesforce, Marketo, Outreach), and experience with AI marketing software and generative AI (GPT, Claude, Gemini) plus workflow automation tools like Make.com, Zapier, and Workato.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, B2B SaaS RevOps expertise, and AI-native GTM focus. Minor gap: role emphasizes marketing operations/martech depth over broader revenue operations leadership, though Omri's Salesforce and pipeline expertise transfer well.

Your need for someone who bridges Salesforce/Marketo expertise with generative AI implementation aligns with how I've been architecting GTM systems to scale without proportional headcount growth.

title: Director, GTM Tech and Automation
description: Ivo, an AI legal contract review platform, seeks a GTM Engineer/Director to build, automate, and operate the systems powering its go-to-market engine, AI-first. Reporting to the VP Revenue Strategy & Operations, you'll own CRM architecture, cross-platform integrations, and workflow automation. Requires 5–10 years in B2B SaaS GTM operations or revenue technology, strong SFDC administration, strong SQL, and experience deploying AI/LLM-powered agent workflows. Familiarity with usage-based revenue models, Mixpanel, PostHog, and AI copilots is valued.

ai_opener: Hi team at saas.group - I came across Ayrshare's Head of Marketing role and while the remote setup and B2B SaaS motion are compelling, I want to be direct: my 15+ years have been laser-focused on RevOps, GTM infrastructure, and revenue metrics rather than marketing execution. That said, if you're looking to tie marketing metrics directly to pipeline, CAC/LTV optimization, and revenue forecasting accuracy, I'd be worth a conversation about how revenue operations can amplify what your marketing team drives.
